      Return Policy
    
    
      
        Custom Pieces
        
          Custom orders are made specifically for you, so returns are only accepted if there is a
          structural defect — meaning something went wrong on our end. If your piece has a craftsmanship
          issue, reach out and we'll make it right.
        
      
      
      
        All Other Items
        
          Not the right fit? Shop items can be returned within 30 days of delivery
          for a refund. Refunds are issued for the item price only — original and return shipping
          costs are not included.
        
      
    
    
      Questions about a return? Reach out at Murraywoodworksllc@gmail.com and we'll sort it out.

title: Murray Wood Works
text:
Form, function, and a little bit of soul.
    Handcrafted in Ohio  ·  Locally sourced hardwood
    
      Shop Now
      See the Work
    
  




  
    Built by hand. Meant to last.
    
      I grew up in my dad's basement shop, learning to fix things long before I ever thought about making them beautiful.
      Somewhere along the way, woodworking stopped being just functional and became something closer to meditation —
      a way to create something lasting with my hands.
    
    
      Every piece starts with solid hardwood locally sourced. As a lifelong environmentalist, its important to me to responsibly source this wood from local mills and arborists who believe in sustainable practices. My goal is to give this wood a second life that is functional, long-lasting and crafted with extreme care. I combine traditional hand techniques with modern methods to create detailed pieces that my customers will appreciate.
    
    
      The goal is always the same: something beautiful, built to last, at a price that's fair.
    
    
    More About the Shop
  




  
    
      "Looking for a gift that stops someone in their tracks?
      Something they weren't expecting — but will immediately love and keep forever?"
    
    Find the Perfect Gift
  




  
    Sourced Locally
    Built in Ohio
    Finished by Hand
    Custom Orders Welcome

title: Handcrafted Wooden Gifts and Furniture
Handcrafted Wooden Gifts and Furniture
About the Shop
text:
About the Shop
  Handcrafted in Ohio  ·  One maker, one piece at a time




  
    
    
      Hi, I'm Andrew.
      
        Growing up, I was always fascinated by all of the tools in my Dad's workshop. From an early age,
         I was always helping my dad fix one thing or another around the house and like my most kids,
          I hated it. But as I got older I began to understand the value of knowing how to fix something yourself, and to appreciate the satisfying feeling of doing something with your own hands instead of asking for help. As I continued to grow my knowledge past my college years, I started to demand more from the things I made. I didn't want to just make something that worked, I wanted to make something that was beautiful, that would last, and that I could be proud of.
      
      
        While I would never consider myself as a 'born artist', I have always loved making things. My goal for this shop is to create pieces that I am proud of and that will be cherished by the people who buy them. I want to make things that are not only functional but also beautiful, and that will last for generations to come. As a sole propiertor business, you can have confidence that every piece is made with care and attention to detail, and that I stand behind my work. I am committed to providing excellent customer service and to creating pieces that you will be excited to own or to gift to your loved ones.
      
      
        I'm always interested in trying out something new or challenging, so if you have an idea for a custom piece, don't hesitate to reach out. I love working with customers to bring their ideas to life and to create something truly unique and special.
      
    
  




  
    
      Wood that means something.
      
        As a lifelong environmentalist, I feel a responsibility to ensure that I source nearly all of my hardwood from small local mills and individual
        craftspeople in the greater Cincinnati area — people who share a commitment to responsible
        land stewardship. While I will use the occasional exotic hardwood for a special piece, my preference is to
        source locally, and to work with wood that was sustainably harvested. Local varieties available include walnut, cherry, maple, oak, cedar and hickory, among others.
      

    
    
  




  
    
      Have something specific in mind? Custom orders are my favorite kind of work —
      bring me a challenge and let's figure it out together.
    
    Get in Touch

title: About
text: Hello! My name is Andrew Murray and I craft unique and custom pieces out of locally and sustainably sourced wood. Whether you’re looking for a one-of-a-kind furniture piece, a handcrafted cutting board, or a personalized gift, I’m here to bring your vision to life. Browse through my Gallery to see some of my previous work, or visit the Shop to explore available items. If you have a specific project in mind or would like to discuss custom orders, please don’t hesitate to Contact me. I look forward to working with you!

title: Caring for Your Piece
text:
On this page, you will find instructions on care for your piece. If you have questions, always feel free to reach out and ask, we’re happy to help!


If you purchased a cutting board or some other food-serving item like a charcuterie tray, we use mineral oil and then a combination of orange oil and beeswax. We do not recommend that you ever wash your board with soap. To clean, wipe off with warm water and towel off to dry. If you scrub the board, avoid using things like steel wool. A sponge, soft-bristled brush, or woven pads are fine. When we finish our boards, we apply mineral oil and wipe it into the wood with a soft cloth. We let that dry for about 30 minutes, then rub in the combination orange oil and beeswax. You can use either of these products or both. Mineral oil can be found at your local drug store, the brand does not matter. Any beeswax or orange oil product is fine too. We typically use Howard’s ‘Feed-N-Wax’, which can be found at most hardware stores.



Howard Feed-N-Wax
